channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Putin says Russia will bolster air defenses in response to Ukrainian drone attacks
item.description: President Vladimir Putin says Russia will strengthen its air defenses to counter recent Ukrainian drone attacks, which have reached deep inside his country and cast a cloud over his showcase economic forum in his hometown of St. Petersburg.
item.content:encoded:
President Vladimir Putin says Russia will strengthen its air defenses to counter recent Ukrainian drone attacks, which have reached deep inside his country and cast a cloud over his showcase economic forum in his hometown of St. Petersburg.

(Image credit: Dmitri Lovetsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: I wrote about George Santos. Then he made a violent threat and lied about it
item.description: NPR reported on new federal investigations examining the former Congressman's bets on the prediction market site Kalshi. Then he threatened the NPR reporter who broke the story.
item.content:encoded:
NPR reported on new federal investigations examining the former Congressman's bets on the prediction market site Kalshi. Then he threatened the NPR reporter who broke the story.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson/AP)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Republicans' sweeping election overhaul fails in the Senate
item.description: The SAVE America Act, a far-reaching Republican election overhaul that President Trump said should be his congressional allies' top priority, has failed in the Senate.
item.content:encoded:
The SAVE America Act, a far-reaching Republican election overhaul that President Trump said should be his congressional allies' top priority, has failed in the Senate.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Miles Parks

item.title: Weakened public health powers raise outbreak risks
item.description: Some jurisdictions have weakened their public health authorities in response to criticism of lockdowns, school closures, mask mandates, vaccine requirements and other COVID-era restrictions.
item.content:encoded:
Some jurisdictions have weakened their public health authorities in response to criticism of lockdowns, school closures, mask mandates, vaccine requirements and other COVID-era restrictions.

(Image credit: PATRICK T. FALLON)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: NTSB says United jet was too slow and too low in Newark landing accident
item.description: Federal investigators say the captain flying the United 767 from Italy was too slow and too low before landing last month at Newark, N.J. The jet struck a light pole, damaging a truck on the turnpike.
item.content:encoded:
Federal investigators say the captain flying the United 767 from Italy was too slow and too low before landing last month at Newark, N.J. The jet struck a light pole, damaging a truck on the turnpike.

(Image credit: Joshua A. Bickel)
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis

item.title: New York City reshapes mass transit system to handle World Cup, NBA finals crowds
item.description: New York transit officials are preparing to handle up to 100,000 extra travelers a day as fans arrive in New York and New Jersey for FIFA World Cup matches.
item.content:encoded:
New York transit officials are preparing to handle up to 100,000 extra travelers a day as fans arrive in New York and New Jersey for FIFA World Cup matches.

(Image credit: ANGELA WEISS/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: North Korea unveils a new plant to produce fuel for nuclear weapons
item.description: State media photos on the place showed it is likely a plant to produce weapons-grade uranium.
item.content:encoded:
State media photos on the place showed it is likely a plant to produce weapons-grade uranium.

(Image credit: Korean Central News Agency)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Embedded: "We Keep Us Safe" from NPR, KUOW and The Seattle Times
item.description: In the summer of 2020, sixteen-year-old Antonio Mays Jr. traveled a thousand miles to join the racial justice movement of his generation. He arrived in Seattle during the Capitol Hill Occupied Protest, known as CHOP. Less than a week later, he was shot and killed there. The case remains unsolved.
item.content:encoded: In the summer of 2020, sixteen-year-old Antonio Mays Jr. traveled a thousand miles to join the racial justice movement of his generation. He arrived in Seattle during the Capitol Hill Occupied Protest, known as CHOP. Less than a week later, he was shot and killed there. The case remains unsolved.

item.title: What will it take to get a vaccine for the Ebola strain driving the current outbreak?
item.description: There is an effective vaccine for Ebola — but not for the variety spreading rapidly in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Trials are going on for several candidates. How long will it take?
item.content:encoded:
There is an effective vaccine for Ebola — but not for the variety spreading rapidly in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Trials are going on for several candidates. How long will it take?

(Image credit: CArol Valade/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Gun control group sues ATF over records release
item.description: Brady, a nonprofit gun control advocacy group, is suing the ATF and the DOJ over their refusals to release documents and other information about who the largest sellers of crime guns in the U.S. are.
item.content:encoded:
Brady, a nonprofit gun control advocacy group, is suing the ATF and the DOJ over their refusals to release documents and other information about who the largest sellers of crime guns in the U.S. are.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz
